subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.1.1: Demonstrate understanding of a variety of songs
indicator_description: Interpret familiar songs
examplars:
1. Identify some familiar songs from learners' locality. [cite: 4026]
2. Teach the songs with themes from their locality. [cite: 4026]
3. Discuss the moral values of the songs. [cite: 4026]
4. Let learners sing songs using gestures appropriately. [cite: 4026]
5. Have learners interpret the songs in their own words. [cite: 4026]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.2.1: Show understanding of a variety of literary pieces.
indicator_description: Interpret rhymes and tongue-twisters in own words
examplars:
1. Revise some familiar rhymes and tongue-twisters learnt. [cite: 4030]
2. Select a rhyme from learners. [cite: 4030]
3. As learners listen and observe, perform the rhyme (Recite it, accompanied with actions and gestures, two or three times). [cite: 4030]
4. Recite lines of the rhyme as learners join in and repeat lines after you. [cite: 4030]
5. Teach the accompanying actions through demonstration. [cite: 4030]
6. Let learners repeat lines of the rhyme on their own accompanied with appropriate actions. [cite: 4030]
7. Through questions, have learners interpret the rhymes in their own words. [cite: 4030]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.4.1: Respond to stories
indicator_description: Identify characters in a story and relate them to real life situations
examplars:
1. Choose a story from learners' background. [cite: 4034]
2. Tell the story with appropriate actions, gestures, facial expressions, etc. [cite: 4034]
3. Have learners retell the story and answer questions on it. [cite: 4034]
4. Let learners say whether or not they have enjoyed the story and why. [cite: 4034]
5. Put learners into groups to identify the characters and their actions and relate them to real life situations. [cite: 4034]
6. NOTE: Use pictures/sketches, real objects, etc. [cite: 4034]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.4.1: Respond to stories
indicator_description: Retell stories sequentially, following story structure (beginning, middle and ending)
examplars:
1. Revise the previous story told by having learners recall some of the characters. [cite: 4034]
2. Discuss the moral lessons/values of the story. [cite: 4034]
3. Let learners retell the story sequentially, following story structure (beginning, middle and ending). [cite: 4034]
4. Let learners relate the story to real life situations. [cite: 4034]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.4.1: Respond to stories
indicator_description: Tell stories from cultural background
examplars:
1. Revise some stories learners have heard. [cite: 4034]
2. Tell the story as learners listen. [cite: 4034]
3. Let learners tell their own stories. [cite: 4034]
4. Discuss the moral lessons/values in stories told. [cite: 4034]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.5.1: Perform Stories
indicator_description: Dramatise/role-play stories heard or read
examplars:
1. Have learners select an interesting story heard or read in class. [cite: 4039]
2. Retell the story. [cite: 4039]
3. Discuss the story and have learners recall main events and characters. [cite: 4039]
4. Select characters and assign them roles to play. [cite: 4039]
5. Let learners dramatise/role-play the story. [cite: 4039]
6. Discuss moral values from the story. [cite: 4039]
7. Have learners say whether or not they have enjoyed the drama and why. [cite: 4039]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.6.1: Use certain culturally acceptable language for communication
indicator_description: Use appropriate greetings for special occasions
examplars:
1. Revise daily greetings by having learners identify greeting times in the day: e.g. Morning-Good morning, Afternoon-Good afternoon, Evening-Good evening. [cite: 4042]
2. Discuss special occasions such as birthdays, anniversaries and festivals. [cite: 4042]
3. Discuss the correct greetings and responses on these occasions. [cite: 4042]
4. Let learners take turns to demonstrate greetings for special occasions and also practise the appropriate responses. [cite: 4042]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.6.1: Explore using certain culturally acceptable language for communication
indicator_description: Talk about events at home
examplars:
1. Introduce the day's topic and show pictures of activities at home. [cite: 4046]
2. Have learners think-pair-share the domestic activities. [cite: 4046]
3. Have learners talk about which of the events they like most and why they like them. [cite: 4046]
4. Have learners role-play some of the scenes. E.g. People engaged in domestic chores. People at table. [cite: 4046]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.6.1: Explore using certain culturally acceptable language for communication
indicator_description: Talk about things and places in their homes
examplars:
1. Ask learners to name things and places in their homes and say what happens at those places. [cite: 4046]
2. Show pictures of important places in their community, such as the chief's palace, the market, the police station, etc. and have learners think-pair-share ideas or views with the person sitting next to them. [cite: 4046]
3. Put learners into groups to talk about their pictures. [cite: 4046]
4. Rotate the pictures until each group has had its turn to talk about all the pictures. [cite: 4046]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.6.1: Explore using certain culturally acceptable language for communication
indicator_description: Use a wide variety of words to talk about manners
examplars: 1. [No specific exemplars provided in text for this indicator]. [cite: 4046]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.6.2: Apply knowledge of language and communicative skills to participate in conversation
indicator_description: Demonstrate turn-taking in conversations
examplars:
1. Use the Community Circle Activity: Sit learners in horse-shoe formation. [cite: 4046]
2. Call individual learners to talk about what happened over the weekend. e. g. events, personal experiences, incidents, etc. [cite: 4046]
3. Let learners take turns to name and describe events of recent occurrence. [cite: 4046]
4. Discuss the importance of turn-taking in conversation: e.g. i. They talk after the last speaker has stopped. ii. They talk when invited e.g. by the group leader. [cite: 4046]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.6.2: Apply knowledge of language and communicative skills to participate in conversation
indicator_description: Ask and answer questions for clarifications about what other people say
examplars:
1. Have learners recall the discussion on turn-taking in conversation and state the rules. [cite: 4049]
2. Encourage learners to ask and answer questions for clarification on what other learners have said. [cite: 4049]
3. Encourage appropriate language use during conversations. [cite: 4049]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Listen to and interact actively with drama
examplars:
1. Before Reading: Activate previous knowledge; discuss illustrations; predict content; teach key vocabulary. [cite: 4050]
2. During Reading: Read text aloud; pause for discussion and simple questions. [cite: 4050]
3. After Reading: Retell story; role-play story. [cite: 4050]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Recognise and relate the sequence of events (beginning, middle and end) in drama
examplars:
1. Read a narrative text aloud to learners. [cite: 4054]
2. Put learners in groups to identify and talk about the parts of the drama (beginning, middle and ending). [cite: 4054]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Recognise and discuss events in a story (characters, settings, moral, etc.)
examplars:
1. Read aloud a story to learners. [cite: 4054]
2. Have them identify and discuss (in pairs) the events in the story. [cite: 4054]
3. Have groups share their views with the whole class. [cite: 4054]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Share facts from texts heard with a partner, groups or the teacher
examplars:
1. Read an information text aloud using the KWL Strategy. [cite: 4054]
2. KWL Strategy: Before reading, think-pair-share what they know (K) and what they want to know (W). During reading, pause for discussion. After reading, share what they have learned. [cite: 4054]
3. Put learners in groups to share facts from the text heard. [cite: 4054]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.8.1: Demonstrate understanding in asking and answering questions
indicator_description: Use appropriate pronunciation and intonation in asking and answering Wh - questions
examplars:
1. Revise Yes/No questions. [cite: 4057]
2. Ask Wh-questions and have learners answer them (e.g. Who took my pen? Where is the book?). [cite: 4057]
3. Identify common Wh-words (what, when, why, how). [cite: 4057]
4. Pair practice using appropriate pronunciation and intonation in turns. [cite: 4057]
5. Write out simple Wh-questions individually. [cite: 4057]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.9.1: Demonstrate understanding in commands, instructions, directions and requests
indicator_description: Give and respond to commands
examplars:
1. Revise verbs by identifying present forms and using them in sentences (e.g. come, go, sit). [cite: 4061]
2. Execute commands (e.g. stand up, close the door). [cite: 4061]
3. Role-play giving and responding to commands. [cite: 4061]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.9.1: Demonstrate understanding in commands, instructions, directions and requests
indicator_description: Make and respond to polite requests
examplars:
1. Demonstrate how to ask for favors in pairs. [cite: 4061]
2. Discuss examples of polite requests (e.g. Can you hold the bag?). [cite: 4061]
3. Discuss polite request words (e.g. Please, could you, do you mind, May I). [cite: 4061]
4. Practice making and responding to requests using "please". [cite: 4061]
5. Discuss the importance of "please". [cite: 4061]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.9.1: Demonstrate understanding in commands, instructions, directions and requests
indicator_description: Respond to commands, instructions and requests
examplars:
1. Respond to typical classroom instructions. [cite: 4065]
2. Practice giving and responding to instructions. [cite: 4065]
3. Role-play scenarios (parents, teachers, prefects) giving/responding to instructions. [cite: 4065]
4. Example: Draw a circle in a square; write 'A' in the circle. [cite: 4065]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.10.1: Plan and present information and ideas for a variety of purposes
indicator_description: Speak with confidence before different audience
examplars:
1. Pair activity to share holiday plans. [cite: 4069]
2. Share partner's plans with the class. [cite: 4069]
3. Use probing questions for more info (e.g. Where will he go first? What will he be doing there?). [cite: 4069]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.1.10.1: Plan and present information and ideas for a variety of purposes
indicator_description: Maintain appropriate posture, eye contact and use appropriate verbal and non-verbal cues to convey meaning
examplars:
1. Model and observe appropriate posture and eye contact. [cite: 4069]
2. Discuss when to use specific cues for different people (e.g. teacher vs friend). [cite: 4069]
3. Role-play maintaining cues in communication (e.g. showing disapproval with crossed arms). [cite: 4069]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.2.1: Connect sounds to letters and blend letters/syllables in order to read and write
indicator_description: Blend syllables to produce words
examplars:
1. Recital linking letters of the alphabet. [cite: 4074]
2. Blend syllables in pairs/groups/individually to form meaningful words. [cite: 4074]
3. Use word families to build words. [cite: 4074]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.2.1: Connect sounds to letters and blend letters/syllables in order to read and write
indicator_description: Use the alphabetic knowledge to decode unknown words (e. g. use beginning and ending consonants, consonant blends, consonant digraphs, vowel sounds, vowel digraphs and word patterns)
examplars:
1. Begin with alphabet song, rhyme, or poem. [cite: 4074]
2. Decode unknown words by changing letter positions (beginning, middle, end). [cite: 4074]
3. Demonstrate that consonant sounds may stay the same when moved. [cite: 4074]
4. Play alphabet games in pairs/groups. [cite: 4074]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.2.1: Connect sounds to letters and blend letters/syllables in order to read and write
indicator_description: Use elements of structural analysis to decode unknown words.
examplars:
1. Analyse basic structure of unknown two-syllable words (e.g. root word 'clean', prefix 'un-', word 'unclean'). [cite: 4077]
2. Identify suffixes (e.g. root word 'slow', suffix '-ly', word 'slowly'). [cite: 4077]
3. Identify features of compound words (e.g. 'class' + 'room' = 'classroom'). [cite: 4077]
4. NOTE: Focus on blends, digraphs, prefixes, suffixes, root words, compound words. [cite: 4077]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.3.1. Employ knowledge of rhyming endings and common digraphs to enhance reading comprehension
indicator_description: Use common rhyming/endings to decode simple words
examplars:
1. Simple word formation by changing beginning letters (pan, man, fan). [cite: 4080]
2. Build on rhyming endings and read in groups. [cite: 4080]
3. Form sentences with rhyming words. [cite: 4080]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.3.1. Employ knowledge of rhyming endings and common digraphs to enhance reading comprehension
indicator_description: Use words containing digraphs to make meaningful sentences.
examplars:
1. Introduce simple digraphs (ch - church, ph - photo, sh - shirt). [cite: 4080]
2. Build words from digraphs. [cite: 4080]
3. Form meaningful sentences with the words. [cite: 4080]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.6.1: Understand word meanings and usages
indicator_description: Read level-appropriate sight words, understand and use many of them in meaningful sentences
examplars:
1. Introduce selected sight words in context on the board. [cite: 4084]
2. Repeat words aloud and read randomly. [cite: 4084]
3. Use think-pair-share to form sentences. [cite: 4084]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.6.1: Understand word meanings and usages
indicator_description: Use context clues to infer meanings of words
examplars:
1. Create a story using specific vocabulary. [cite: 4084]
2. Use context to find word meanings. [cite: 4084]
3. Make sentences using the key words. [cite: 4084]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.6.1: Understand word meanings and usages
indicator_description: Show awareness that homonyms have different meanings in different contexts
examplars: 1. Provide examples in context (e.g. 'right' as in correct vs direction; 'bat' as in animal vs sports equipment). [cite: 4084]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.6.1: Understand word meanings and usages
indicator_description: Use dictionaries and glossaries to clarify meaning of words
examplars:
1. Introduce picture dictionaries and glossaries. [cite: 4084]
2. Use guide words to find meanings. [cite: 4084]
3. Practice in pairs. [cite: 4084]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.7.1: Understand and analyse texts read
indicator_description: Use illustrations, text clues and context to make predictions during reading
examplars:
1. Use titles, illustrations, and clues to make meaning. [cite: 4088]
2. Predict and brainstorm on topic, then read to confirm. [cite: 4088]
3. Pause frequently during reading to predict and enhance comprehension. [cite: 4088]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.7.1: Understand and analyse texts read
indicator_description: Ask and answer factual and inferential questions about level-appropriate texts
examplars:
1. Read level-appropriate texts in pairs. [cite: 4088]
2. Ask and answer simple inferential questions. [cite: 4088]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.7.1: Understand and analyse texts read
indicator_description: Retell level-appropriate texts in own words
examplars:
1. Revise previous stories. [cite: 4088]
2. Retell using simple herring-bone strategies. [cite: 4088]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.7.2: Use knowledge skills and techniques of reading to understand and interpret texts
indicator_description: Demonstrate understanding of the purpose and features of information texts
examplars: 1. Read informational texts to identify purpose and features via think-pair-share. [cite: 4092]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.7.2: Use knowledge skills and techniques of reading to understand and interpret texts
indicator_description: Describe events in a story
examplars:
1. Retell stories sequentially. [cite: 4092]
2. Identify and describe events. [cite: 4092]
3. Guide learners to unfold events sequentially. [cite: 4092]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.7.2: Use knowledge skills and techniques of reading to understand and interpret texts
indicator_description: Connect characters, ideas and information within a text
examplars: 1. Connect ideas and information within a text to help construct meaning. [cite: 4092]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.7.2: Use knowledge skills and techniques of reading to understand and interpret texts
indicator_description: Read level-appropriate texts with little support
examplars:
1. Read texts with minimal teacher support. [cite: 4092]
2. Gradually scaffold toward independent reading. [cite: 4092]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.7.2: Use knowledge skills and techniques of reading to understand and interpret texts
indicator_description: Use visualisation strategy (form mental images about texts) to enhance understanding of level-appropriate texts
examplars:
1. Model using visualisation to construct meaning. [cite: 4092]
2. Guide practice of forming mental images to understand texts. [cite: 4092]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.9.1: Read texts fluently
indicator_description: Read texts/stories/passages with good pace, accuracy and expression
examplars:
1. Model reading aloud for pace and expression. [cite: 4095]
2. Group/pair reading of short texts with appropriate pace and expression. [cite: 4095]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.9.1: Read texts fluently
indicator_description: Read and recognise 120 sight words
examplars:
1. Introduce more sight words in context. [cite: 4095]
2. Identify and use at least 120 sight-words (focus on a few at a time). [cite: 4095]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.2.9.1: Read texts fluently
indicator_description: Use contextual clues to confirm or self-correct pronunciation while reading aloud
examplars:
1. Use word context to confirm or self-correct pronunciation. [cite: 4095]
2. Check intonation while reading. [cite: 4095]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.4.2.1: Copy and rewrite sentences correctly
indicator_description: Copy sentences clearly
examplars:
1. Demonstrate copying on board. [cite: 4099]
2. Practice legibility on board or in books. [cite: 4099]
3. Copy sentences from a book. [cite: 4099]
4. Focus on spacing, alignment, and clarity. [cite: 4099]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.4.3.1: Use general skills, strategies and knowledge of letter sounds to enable them write legibly and boldly
indicator_description: Copy words in lower and upper case using correct spacing
examplars:
1. Write letters in upper/lower cases. [cite: 4102]
2. Observe teacher copying words from reading books. [cite: 4102]
3. Read out words from the list. [cite: 4102]
4. Copy words, checking spelling, formation, and spacing. [cite: 4102]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.4.3.1: Use general skills, strategies and knowledge of letters to enable them write legibly and boldly
indicator_description: Use lower and upper case letters appropriately in words and simple sentences
examplars:
1. Choose topics (My Family, My Pet). [cite: 4106]
2. Talk about topic; teacher writes sentences on board. [cite: 4106]
3. Read sentences (e.g. My father is a teacher). [cite: 4106]
4. Rewrite sentences with corrected capitalization (e.g. my Father -> My father). [cite: 4106]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.4.3.1: Use general skills, strategies and knowledge of letters to enable them write legibly and boldly
indicator_description: Space words appropriately in sentences
examplars:
1. Copy sentences from board, paying attention to spacing. [cite: 4106]
2. Display work. [cite: 4106]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.4.4.1: Draw and label simple pictures
indicator_description: Draw and label objects found in their environment
examplars:
1. Observe labeled object charts. [cite: 4109]
2. Group activity to pick environmental areas (home, market) and list objects. [cite: 4109]
3. Draw and label objects from chosen area. [cite: 4109]
4. Edit for spelling and spacing; display for peer review. [cite: 4109]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.4.5.1: Use knowledge of grammar and vocabulary to write words and sentences correctly.
indicator_description: Write simple sentences to express feelings
examplars:
1. Share feelings in groups. [cite: 4113]
2. Write at least one sentence (e.g. "I am happy."). [cite: 4113]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.4.5.1: Use knowledge of grammar and vocabulary to write words and sentences correctly.
indicator_description: Write simple and meaningful sentences on objects found in the environment
examplars:
1. Observation field-trip. [cite: 4113]
2. Discuss trip and write observations (e.g. "I saw a big bus."). [cite: 4113]
3. Group writing of sentences on environmental objects. [cite: 4113]
4. Add pictures to sentences and publish for gallery walk. [cite: 4113]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.4.5.1: Use knowledge of grammar and vocabulary to write words and sentences correctly.
indicator_description: Draw a picture and write at least a sentence to share an opinion
examplars:
1. Study and interpret 'smiley' face images. [cite: 4117]
2. Discuss opinions in pairs. [cite: 4117]
3. NOTE: Accept any interpretation the learner gives. [cite: 4117]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.4.7.1: Write simple compositions
indicator_description: Write or copy correct sentences from substitution tables
examplars:
1. Identify words in 3-4 column substitution table (e.g. We + Play + at + the park). [cite: 4120]
2. Form sentences from table; read aloud and write into books. [cite: 4120]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.4.7.1: Write simple compositions
indicator_description: Match parts of sentences to compose meaningful texts
examplars:
1. Discuss topic (My House) using descriptive sentences. [cite: 4120]
2. Write broken sentences (e.g. "My house" + "is big."). [cite: 4120]
3. Match beginnings to correct endings; perform peer editing. [cite: 4120]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.4.7.2: Matching parts of sentences to composition
indicator_description: Re-arrange jumbled sentences logically
examplars:
1. Tell/Read an interesting story. [cite: 4124]
2. Retell the story. [cite: 4124]
3. Rearrange jumbled story sentences logically. [cite: 4124]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.4.8.1: Compose short paragraphs through answering questions
indicator_description: Develop two coherent paragraphs on one idea or concepts using leading questions
examplars:
1. Brainstorm ideas on topic (e.g. "Myself"). [cite: 4127]
2. Group activity using leading questions to develop ideas into one or two paragraphs. [cite: 4127]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.4.10.1: Narrate situations, express feelings and convey point of view about the real or fictional world
indicator_description: Write about real or imagined experiences or events using the process approach
examplars:
1. Question daily routines. [cite: 4129]
2. Brainstorm and choose topic (e.g. "A visit to the market"). [cite: 4129]
3. Assist writing paragraphs using experience questions (Where is the market? What did you buy?). [cite: 4129]
4. Write and post short paragraph on notice board. [cite: 4129]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.4.12.1: Demonstrate knowledge of description in writing
indicator_description: Use simple sentences to describe feelings
examplars:
1. Name and describe common objects using adjectives. [cite: 4134]
2. Demonstrate describing an object in 3-5 sentences on the board. [cite: 4134]
3. Individuals describe objects orally and then in writing. [cite: 4134]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.5.1.1: Apply knowledge of capitalisation in writing
indicator_description: Use capital letter to write names of particular places and days of the week
examplars:
1. Identify names of places and days in sample text. [cite: 4136]
2. Compare initial letters of proper vs common names. [cite: 4136]
3. Rewrite text with capitalized proper names. [cite: 4136]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.5.1.1: Apply knowledge of capitalisation in writing
indicator_description: Use capital letters to begin the first words of sentences
examplars: 1. Practice beginning sentences with capitals (e.g. "Kofi is a good boy."). [cite: 4141]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.5.2.1: Apply knowledge of punctuation in written communication
indicator_description: Use full stops at the end of sentences and question marks at the end of questions
examplars:
1. Observe sentence beginnings and endings in readers. [cite: 4139]
2. Demonstrate full stop (.) use; copy and punctuate sentences. [cite: 4139]
3. Present and practice question mark use similarly. [cite: 4139]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.5.4.1: Apply knowledge of action words in communication
indicator_description: Use the simple present tense to express habitual actions (simple truths, interest, feelings and opinions)
examplars:
1. Share simple daily activities (e.g. "I read stories everyday."). [cite: 4140]
2. Create situational practice (simple truths, interests, feelings, opinions). [cite: 4140]
3. Introduce drills and small group discussions using the structure. [cite: 4140]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.5.5.1: Apply knowledge of adjectives in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use simple sentences to describe the weather and show quantity and position or order of people and things
examplars: 1. Use texts to describe weather ("It is cold."), quantity ("I have two pens."), and order ("She is on the first row."). [cite: 4146]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.5.7.1: Understand and identify the use of prepositions
indicator_description: Identify prepositions in sentences to indicate days, dates and places
examplars: 1. Use 'in' and 'on' in context (e.g. "I saw a bird on Monday."). [cite: 4147]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.5.7.1: Understand and identify the use of prepositions
indicator_description: Use prepositions to form simple sentences to indicate position and time
examplars: 1. Identify prepositions in short passages indicating position and time (e.g. "Esi sat on a chair in the room."). [cite: 4147, 4153]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.5.9.1: Understand simple sentences in writing
indicator_description: Identify the structure of simple sentences
examplars: 1. Use context to identify structure (Name + Doing word, e.g. "Musa ate."). [cite: 4152]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.5.9.1: Understand simple sentences in writing
indicator_description: Identify nouns and verbs in simple sentences
examplars: 1. Use questions to identify nouns and verbs (Who danced? What did Ofori do?). [cite: 4152]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.5.10.1: Use phonics knowledge to spell words
indicator_description: Use phonics knowledge to spell words with irregular phonic letter pattern
examplars:
1. Play pick and spell for irregular words (e.g. 'the', 'face'). [cite: 4155]
2. Use words in oral and written sentences. [cite: 4155]
3. Fill in blanks in sentences using learned words. [cite: 4159]

subject: English Language
content_standard: B2.6.1.1: Read widely for pleasure and to demonstrate independent reading and learning in the literary area
indicator_description: Read a variety of age and level-appropriate books and texts from print and non-print
examplars:
1. Use Author's chair for reading time. [cite: 4162]
2. Select from age-appropriate narratives, pop-ups, flip-the-page texts, and e-books. [cite: 4162]
3. Guide learners in book selection for each session. [cite: 4162]
